claim: No Stable, file-safety, transport, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: File identity is text; actions are native buttons; progress is native progress; status is a bounded live region.
semantics.naming: Every action includes the visible file name through its named group; consumer action labels are localizable.
semantics.roleOverride: The attachment is not made clickable as a whole and no file type is inferred as safe.

interaction.keyboard: Enabled action buttons follow native Tab, Space, and Enter behavior.
interaction.pointer: Preview, download, and removal are separate explicit targets.
interaction.touch: Action controls retain the shared preferred target size and wrap at narrow widths.
interaction.speech: Visible action labels and file identity create deterministic speech targets.
interaction.switchAccess: Actions remain separate sequential scan stops with no timed requirement.

preferences.forcedColors: Canvas, CanvasText, ButtonFace, ButtonText, and Highlight preserve boundaries and focus.
preferences.reducedMotion: No meaning depends on motion; all descendant motion duration is removed.

responsive.overflowPolicy: File names wrap and actions reflow without clipping.

consumerResponsibilities: Validate signature, type, malware policy, authorization, and safe disposition before preview or download.
Own transport, retry, persistence, URL creation, download, deletion confirmation, and irreversible effects.
Localize status, action, and safety language and do not describe an unverified file as safe.
limitations: Attachment never reads, scans, previews, downloads, uploads, persists, or deletes file bytes.
Manual assistive-technology, packed-consumer, lifecycle, and generated parity evidence remains unrecorded.
